slides.title: The Keyof Operator 🔑
slides.content: The `keyof` operator is incredibly powerful. It takes an object type and cleanly extracts all of its keys as a union of literal strings.

slides.title: Extracting Keys
slides.content: Notice how `keyof User` automatically transforms into the precise union `'id' | 'username' | 'email'`.

quizData.question: If you dynamically add `role: string` to the `User` interface, what intelligently happens to the `UserKey` extracted type above?

options.text: It fails and you must manually rewrite the UserKey alias.
It automatically updates to include 'role'. The dynamic union instantly becomes `'id' | 'username' | 'email' | 'role'`.
It throws a recursive compiler error.

quizData.explanation: `keyof` behaves dynamically. As the master interface scales, anything utilizing `keyof` structurally inherits the new keys automatically.

slides.title: Non-Null Assertion Operator ❗️
slides.content: Another highly common advanced trick is the `!` operator. It structurally commands TypeScript: *"I promise this specific variable is NOT null or undefined right now."*

slides.title: Danger Zone ☢️
slides.content: Just exactly like the `as` keyword, appending `!` lies to the compiler. If the value randomly turns out to genuinely be null at runtime, your entire app explicitly crashes! Use with extreme caution.
